purpose: A source-bounded library of recurring executive decision structures for controlled evaluation and future integration.

may_do: suggest evidence categories and executive questions
help preserve options and articulate trade-offs
must_not_do: supply case facts absent from the current source
trigger backpressure from a pattern match alone
assign an accountability owner without evidence
select or recommend the final strategic pathway
be represented as active runtime behavior
knowledge_boundary.pattern_match_requirement: At least two independent source anchors and no stronger competing interpretation.
knowledge_boundary.backpressure_requirement: A real decision or commitment request plus a critical missing or contradictory evidence, accountability, governance, financial, compliance, or safety boundary.

external_framework_candidates.name: G20/OECD Principles of Corporate Governance 2023
external_framework_candidates.intended_use: General governance and board oversight orientation.

external_framework_candidates.name: COSO Enterprise Risk Management
external_framework_candidates.intended_use: Risk, strategy, performance, and oversight orientation.

external_framework_candidates.name: OSHA Process Safety Management
external_framework_candidates.intended_use: Process-safety orientation only; jurisdiction-specific applicability must be verified.

patterns.business_context: A core growth thesis is weakening or a new market, product, or adjacency requires a material shift in attention and resources.
decision_signals: request to approve entry, exit, pivot, or material resource reallocation
evidence that the current growth thesis no longer explains observed performance
commitment to a new market before capability or demand assumptions are validated
informational_signals: market update without an approval request
early opportunity scan with no capital or operating commitment
scenario discussion explicitly described as exploratory
prior_assumption_archetypes: the current core can sustain the planned growth rate
the adjacency is reachable with existing capabilities
market entry can precede local operating readiness
material_option_structures: defend and optimize the core
shift materially toward the adjacency
stage a bounded entry while preserving exit options
defer commitment and gather discriminating evidence
trade_off_structures: near-term core economics versus future growth optionality
speed of entry versus evidence and capability maturity
focus versus portfolio diversification
common_missing_evidence: no source-grounded reason the old growth thesis changed
no comparison between internal capability and entry requirements
no downside or exit condition
no criteria for shifting resources from the core
accountability_questions: Who owns the market thesis and its validation?
Who can stop or resize the commitment when assumptions fail?
Which forum owns capital reallocation across the core and adjacency?
governance_safety_boundaries: Do not present exploratory market signals as validated demand.
Do not treat a staged experiment as authorization for irreversible expansion.
backpressure_conditions: A material entry or reallocation is requested but demand, capability, or downside assumptions needed for that commitment are unsupported.
The requested pivot is effectively irreversible and no accountability or exit boundary is defined.
decision_scope_potentially_supportable_n: approve a bounded evidence-gathering step when its scope and learning criteria are explicit
align on decision criteria without selecting the final market pathway
commitment_scope_not_yet_supportable: full market commitment based only on exploratory signals
irreversible core resource withdrawal without downside analysis
meeting_room_question_templates: Which assumption about the core or the new market has changed enough to justify reallocating scarce attention and capital?
What evidence would distinguish a reversible learning step from an irreversible strategic pivot?
patterns.one_line_decision_cue_template: The decision is not whether the opportunity is attractive; it is how much of the current engine we are willing to put at risk before the new thesis is proven.
prohibited_inferences: a large market automatically implies an attainable market
a staged option is risk-free
the agent may choose the winning market

patterns.business_context: Executives must acquire, combine, retain, separate, or exit an asset or business under valuation, integration, regulatory, and timing uncertainty.
decision_signals: request to sign, close, bid, divest, or terminate
approval of valuation range or transaction structure
acceptance of regulatory, integration, or stranded-cost exposure
informational_signals: target screening without transaction authority
portfolio performance review with no disposition request
status update on diligence already authorized
prior_assumption_archetypes: the asset is worth more inside the portfolio than outside it
synergies can be captured within the planned integration window
regulatory clearance and closing conditions are manageable
material_option_structures: proceed under the current structure
proceed with contingencies, staged ownership, or revised terms
defer pending discriminating evidence
exit the transaction or divestment path
trade_off_structures: deal speed versus diligence and conditional protection
strategic control versus capital and integration exposure
near-term transaction certainty versus long-term value capture
common_missing_evidence: synergy stated without owner, timing, or dependency
single-point valuation without downside range
regulatory condition treated as procedural rather than outcome-relevant
no cost or consequence of delay and exit
accountability_questions: Who owns each material synergy or separation obligation?
Who is authorized to renegotiate or stop before closing?
Which forum accepts residual regulatory and integration risk?
governance_safety_boundaries: Do not imply legal or regulatory clearance from commercial momentum.
Do not convert management enthusiasm into transaction certainty.
backpressure_conditions: Signing or closing is requested while a material diligence, regulatory, valuation, or accountability boundary remains unresolved.
The transaction structure makes a major exposure irreversible before the stated condition is tested.
decision_scope_potentially_supportable_n: approve bounded diligence or negotiation parameters
align on conditions that must be met before signing or closing
commitment_scope_not_yet_supportable: unconditional closing with unresolved material conditions
final valuation acceptance without a defensible range
meeting_room_question_templates: Which unresolved condition could change the transaction from value-creating to value-destructive?
What protection or stopping condition is required before commercial momentum becomes irreversible commitment?
patterns.one_line_decision_cue_template: The decision is not whether the transaction has strategic logic; it is which unresolved exposure the organization is willing to own to preserve timing and control.
prohibited_inferences: management sponsorship proves synergy realization
commercial agreement implies regulatory approval
the agent may authorize or reject the transaction

patterns.business_context: A major project, plant, platform, or aging asset requires investment, replacement, life extension, sequencing, or retirement.
decision_signals: request to approve capital, phase-gate release, replacement, or retirement
material change in asset condition, project economics, or delivery risk
commitment that narrows future options or creates stranded exposure
informational_signals: routine asset-health update without funding authority
project progress report within an approved envelope
early concept screening before gate criteria are set
prior_assumption_archetypes: the asset can meet future requirements with normal maintenance
the project remains within the approved cost, schedule, and benefit thesis
replacement timing can be deferred without material reliability or value loss
material_option_structures: continue maintaining or life-extending the current asset
replace or reinvest at full scope
phase or modularize the commitment
retire, divest, or redesign the operating requirement
trade_off_structures: near-term cash protection versus reliability and future capacity
full-scope economics versus staged flexibility
speed versus design maturity and execution confidence
minimum_evidence_categories: cost, schedule, benefit, and sensitivity range
stage gates, owner, and stop conditions
common_missing_evidence: benefit estimate lacks baseline
maintenance and replacement options are not compared on the same horizon
no explicit consequence of deferral
no gate for releasing later irreversible spend
accountability_questions: Who owns the benefit case after capital approval?
Who controls scope changes and gate release?
Who can halt operation or execution when the boundary is breached?
governance_safety_boundaries: Do not treat sunk cost as evidence that the next tranche should proceed.
Do not infer safe continued operation from the absence of a recent incident.
backpressure_conditions: Irreversible capital or continued operation is requested without a credible baseline, alternatives, or material safety boundary.
The decision depends on benefits or asset condition that cannot be traced to evidence.
decision_scope_potentially_supportable_n: approve a bounded diagnostic, design, or early gate
align on criteria for full capital release
commitment_scope_not_yet_supportable: full release based on unbaselined benefits
continued high-consequence operation with an unresolved safety limit
meeting_room_question_templates: Which assumption about asset life, project economics, or future requirement has changed enough to alter the capital path?
What can be learned or protected through staging before the next irreversible commitment?
patterns.one_line_decision_cue_template: The decision is not only how much capital to spend; it is how much reliability, flexibility, and downside exposure the organization is willing to trade across time.
prohibited_inferences: prior spend validates future spend
no recent failure means the asset boundary is safe
the agent may release capital

patterns.business_context: Executives must respond to cost, demand, mix, pricing, or growth pressure when not all economic objectives can be protected simultaneously.
decision_signals: request to change price, discount, capacity, segment focus, or growth investment
evidence that margin or volume assumptions are structurally changing
approval of a commercial pilot or broad rollout
informational_signals: periodic sales or margin update with no policy request
market research readout before a commercial decision
variance explanation within an approved commercial plan
prior_assumption_archetypes: cost pressure is temporary and can be absorbed
volume will remain stable through a price change
growth can be funded without material margin dilution
material_option_structures: protect margin through price, mix, or discount discipline
protect volume through selective absorption or investment
run a bounded segment or channel pilot
redesign the offer or cost-to-serve
trade_off_structures: margin versus volume
short-term share versus price integrity
broad rollout speed versus segment-level learning
common_missing_evidence: average economics hide segment differences
benefit stated without customer behavior evidence
no measure of cannibalization or discount leakage
no distinction between pilot and enterprise-wide commitment
accountability_questions: Who owns the price-volume thesis by segment?
Who monitors unintended discount or churn effects?
Who can stop or resize a rollout when pilot conditions fail?
governance_safety_boundaries: Do not claim that margin and volume can both be fully protected when the source shows a constrained cost curve.
Do not generalize a pilot result beyond the measured segment without evidence.
backpressure_conditions: A broad commercial commitment is requested without a baseline or evidence about customer response that is critical to the decision.
The proposed framing hides an unavoidable economic trade-off.
decision_scope_potentially_supportable_n: align on segment-level decision criteria and guardrails
commitment_scope_not_yet_supportable: enterprise-wide rollout from untested average assumptions
claiming a win-win without source-grounded economics
meeting_room_question_templates: Which customer or segment behavior would prove that the current price-volume assumption no longer holds?
What are we willing to sacrifice—margin, volume, speed, or scope—and where should that sacrifice be bounded?
patterns.one_line_decision_cue_template: The decision is not whether growth and margin both matter; it is which economic objective can be protected now and which risk must be explicitly owned.
prohibited_inferences: average demand represents every segment
a price move has no behavioral consequence
the agent may set commercial policy

patterns.business_context: The organization must balance cost, service, concentration, inventory, supplier commitment, and recovery capability under disruption risk.
decision_signals: request to approve sole-source, dual-source, inventory, localization, or long-term commitment
material disruption changes the reliability assumption
cost advantage depends on concentration or reduced redundancy
informational_signals: supplier performance update without a sourcing decision
scenario exercise with no contract or inventory request
routine service-level reporting
prior_assumption_archetypes: the lowest-cost source is sufficiently reliable
inventory and recovery capacity can absorb disruption
supplier concentration does not create material strategic exposure
material_option_structures: retain concentrated supply and accept exposure
qualify or contract a second source
build inventory or recovery buffers
redesign product, network, or demand to reduce dependency
trade_off_structures: unit cost versus resilience
supplier commitment versus switching flexibility
working capital versus continuity protection
common_missing_evidence: no quantified or bounded disruption consequence
second-source option named without qualification lead time
inventory buffer treated as equivalent to structural resilience
contract commitment lacks exit or failure condition
accountability_questions: Who owns the concentration risk and recovery plan?
Who can activate alternate supply or inventory release?
Which forum accepts the residual service and cost exposure?
governance_safety_boundaries: Do not infer resilience from historical supplier performance alone.
Do not present an unqualified alternate supplier as immediately available capacity.
backpressure_conditions: A long-term or exclusive commitment is requested while recovery, alternative capacity, or exit boundaries critical to continuity are unknown.
The decision creates material operational or safety exposure with no accountable response owner.
decision_scope_potentially_supportable_n: approve supplier qualification or a bounded resilience test
align on acceptable concentration and recovery criteria
commitment_scope_not_yet_supportable: exclusive commitment without recovery and exit evidence
claiming continuity protection from an untested alternative
meeting_room_question_templates: What disruption duration or service consequence would make the current cost advantage unacceptable?
Which resilience mechanism buys real recovery capacity rather than the appearance of redundancy?
patterns.one_line_decision_cue_template: The decision is not whether concentration lowers cost; it is how much continuity risk the organization is willing to own for that advantage.
prohibited_inferences: past reliability proves future continuity
inventory alone eliminates structural dependency
the agent may select the supplier

patterns.business_context: Operational continuity, production, or project timing collides with a material safety, environmental, regulatory, or community boundary.
decision_signals: request to continue, restart, suspend, or expand high-consequence operation
breach or near-breach of a safety, environmental, permit, or community condition
production or schedule pressure conflicts with a stated operating boundary
informational_signals: routine safety performance update with no operating decision
closed incident review with no unresolved boundary
training or assurance update within approved controls
prior_assumption_archetypes: existing controls are sufficient for the planned operating envelope
the event is isolated rather than evidence of a systemic weakness
production can continue while remediation occurs
material_option_structures: continue within a verified safe boundary
reduce rate or scope under temporary controls
stand down until specified evidence or remediation exists
redesign or retire the hazardous activity
trade_off_structures: production continuity versus safety or license integrity
temporary control versus permanent remediation
speed of restart versus verification confidence
minimum_evidence_categories: named human authority for operation and stop-work
common_missing_evidence: root cause or hazard boundary remains unresolved
temporary control lacks effectiveness evidence
restart authority and halt criteria are unclear
production impact is quantified but safety consequence is not
accountability_questions: Who has authority to continue, reduce, restart, and stop?
Who independently verifies that the boundary is restored?
Which accountable forum accepts residual risk?
governance_safety_boundaries: Safety and regulatory applicability must be verified in the operating jurisdiction.
The agent must not authorize operation, restart, or risk acceptance.
backpressure_conditions: Continuation or restart is requested while a material safety, environmental, permit, or stop-work boundary is unresolved.
Human authority for accepting, monitoring, or halting the exposure is unclear.
decision_scope_potentially_supportable_n: approve evidence gathering, independent verification, or bounded remediation
define the conditions an accountable human forum must verify before restart
commitment_scope_not_yet_supportable: continue or restart beyond a verified operating boundary
accept residual high-consequence risk without accountable human authority
meeting_room_question_templates: What evidence and accountable authority are required before operation can remain within a defensible boundary?
Which production consequence are we prepared to accept rather than normalize an unresolved safety or license-to-operate exposure?
patterns.one_line_decision_cue_template: The decision is not whether continuity has value; it is whether that value can be pursued without crossing an unresolved safety or license-to-operate boundary.
prohibited_inferences: absence of injury proves the boundary is acceptable
temporary controls guarantee safe operation
the agent may approve restart or risk acceptance

patterns.canonical_name: Technology, AI, Data, and Cyber Governance
patterns.business_context: Technology capability, AI autonomy, data access, or cyber exposure changes how work is executed, trusted, monitored, or governed.
decision_signals: request to scale, deploy, connect, automate, or expand data access
system moves from retrieval or analysis toward recommending or executing action
technology investment changes material operating or accountability boundaries
informational_signals: technology landscape update with no deployment request
proof-of-concept result without authority to scale
cyber or data awareness update with no control decision
prior_assumption_archetypes: the technology is primarily a productivity or infrastructure initiative
human review is sufficient to contain automated error
existing data access and control models extend safely to the new use case
material_option_structures: scale broadly under the proposed operating model
delay until specified controls mature
run bounded low-risk or low-autonomy pilots with explicit governance
redesign the use case to reduce operative agency or data exposure
trade_off_structures: speed and value capture versus control and auditability
autonomy versus human accountability
data breadth versus confidentiality and purpose limitation
feature velocity versus resilience and technical debt
minimum_evidence_categories: use-case value baseline and auditable measure
data access, confidentiality, quality, and lineage
degree of evaluative and operative agency
human approval, monitoring, escalation, and halt rules
error, misuse, cyber, and recovery scenarios
common_missing_evidence: productivity claim lacks a baseline
decision support and autonomous action are not distinguished
accountability for wrong or stale output is unclear
data classes, approvals, monitoring, and halt rules are undefined
accountability_questions: Who owns the use-case outcome and residual risk?
Who approves access, monitors output, updates knowledge, and can halt the system?
Where must a human remain the accountable decision maker?
governance_safety_boundaries: Do not infer production readiness from platform readiness.
Do not treat a human in the workflow as effective oversight without authority, information, and time to intervene.
The agent must not approve deployment, data access, or autonomy.
backpressure_conditions: Scale or material autonomy is requested while accountability, data access, monitoring, or halt boundaries critical to the use case are missing.
A high-consequence use case is requested without a defensible human control and recovery path.
decision_scope_potentially_supportable_n: approve bounded low-risk learning with explicit access, monitoring, and halt boundaries
align on autonomy tiers and the evidence required for each tier
commitment_scope_not_yet_supportable: broad autonomous deployment without accountability and halt ownership
claims of ROI without a credible baseline
meeting_room_question_templates: What level of autonomy and data access are we willing to scale now, given the maturity of accountability, evidence, monitoring, and halt rules?
Which value can be learned through a bounded use case without silently changing the operating model?
patterns.one_line_decision_cue_template: The decision is not whether the technology can create value; it is how much agency and exposure the organization can responsibly scale before its governance is ready.
prohibited_inferences: technical availability equals organizational readiness
a human review step automatically guarantees accountability
the agent may approve deployment or select the autonomy level

patterns.business_context: A restructuring, shared-service move, platform transformation, or new operating model changes authority, capability, sequencing, and business continuity.
decision_signals: request to approve target operating model, phase, funding, or organization change
benefit realization depends on authority, process, data, and behavior changing together
transition risk or dual-running cost becomes material
informational_signals: transformation progress update within an approved phase
organization health review without redesign authority
future-state concept discussion with no commitment request
prior_assumption_archetypes: technology or organization design is the primary constraint
benefits will arrive as soon as the new structure or platform goes live
the business can absorb transition and dual-running load without material performance loss
material_option_structures: move rapidly to the target model
phase by capability, business unit, or process
run a bounded parallel model before migration
redesign scope or defer until prerequisites are met
trade_off_structures: transformation speed versus continuity and adoption
central consistency versus local responsiveness
early benefit ambition versus transition capacity
feature delivery versus technical debt and resilience
minimum_evidence_categories: phase gates, measures, and rollback conditions
common_missing_evidence: target chart is presented without decision rights
benefits are not tied to changed behavior or process
transition capacity and dual-running load are absent
phase gates measure activity rather than outcome
accountability_questions: Who owns the business outcome rather than the implementation activity?
Who resolves cross-functional decision-right conflicts?
Who can pause or resize a phase when continuity or adoption conditions fail?
governance_safety_boundaries: Do not infer operating-model readiness from technical go-live readiness.
Do not present a reporting-line change as a complete accountability model.
backpressure_conditions: A major operating-model or transformation commitment is requested while decision rights, transition capacity, or business-continuity boundaries are critically unclear.
Benefits required to justify the commitment have no source-grounded baseline or accountable owner.
decision_scope_potentially_supportable_n: approve a bounded phase with explicit outcome and stop criteria
align on decision rights and prerequisites before full migration
commitment_scope_not_yet_supportable: enterprise-wide migration without transition and accountability evidence
benefit commitment based only on implementation milestones
meeting_room_question_templates: Which operating constraint—not merely which technology or reporting line—must change for the benefit to exist?
What phase boundary protects continuity while still generating evidence that the target model works?
patterns.one_line_decision_cue_template: The decision is not only what the future model should be; it is how much transition load and continuity risk the organization can absorb while reaching it.
prohibited_inferences: go-live equals benefit realization
an organization chart defines accountability
the agent may choose the target operating model

experimental_candidates.name: Vertically Integrated Value and Transfer-Pricing Visibility
experimental_candidates.reason_held: Promising management-accounting structure, but tax, legal, and jurisdictional boundaries require authoritative specialist review before it becomes an active pattern. A public regression case may still test source-faithful framing without asserting tax rules.

experimental_candidates.name: Related-Party Transaction Oversight
experimental_candidates.reason_held: Material governance pattern with strong jurisdiction and listing-rule dependence; requires primary-source validation and careful conflict-of-interest boundaries.

experimental_candidates.name: Technical Debt versus Feature Velocity
experimental_candidates.reason_held: Useful sub-pattern already represented inside technology and transformation families; held separately until evidence shows that independent routing improves quality rather than taxonomy size.

experimental_candidates.name: Workforce Capacity, Automation, and Social Transition
experimental_candidates.reason_held: High-value real-world pattern, but requires stronger labor, employment, inclusion, and jurisdiction-specific evidence than the current research pack provides.
